# Project Instructions

## Code Styles
- Include type hints in JSDoc comments for JavaScript files.
- Use 2 spaces for indentation.
- If the project uses TypeScript, prefer type annotations over JSDoc comments.
- Use camelCase for variable and function names, PascalCase for classes and interfaces, and UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants.

## Project-specific Guidelines
- All files should include a header comment with the file's purpose and any relevant information.
- For React projects, use functional components and hooks instead of class components.
- Log all errors to a centralized logging service, and avoid using `console.log` in production code.
- Write unit tests for all new features and bug fixes, and aim for at least 80% code coverage.
